title: Cylinder Head Structure Description - Hydraulic Tappet

blocks.text: Cylinder Head Structure Description - Hydraulic Tappet

Hydraulic tappets are used for the valve actuation system. This system achieves quietness, low vibration, and reduced friction. It is also maintenance-free while maintaining optimum valve clearance at all times. When the camshaft rotates, the rocker arm opens and closes the valve using the plunger portion of the hydraulic tappet as a fulcrum. When the rocker arm force does not act on the plunger, the spring pushes the plunger up to optimize the clearance between the rocker arm and camshaft. When the rocker arm pressure acts on the plunger due to bubble lift, the rocker arm is supported by the hydraulic pressure generated by the check ball working to seal the oil chamber.
